name: Indian Apple Chutney
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel, core, and coarsely chop apples.
2. Put the chopped apples, onions, garlic, and raisins into a saucepan.
3. Add salt, sugar, vinegar, cayenne pepper, ground cumin, ground ginger, mustard seeds, dry mustard, and tomato paste to the saucepan.
4. Mix well until all ingredients are combined.
5. Heat gently, stirring occasionally to dissolve the sugar.
6. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
7. Stir in the tomato paste and continue cooking for an additional 7-8 minutes, or until the mixture reaches a thick consistency with very little free liquid.
8. Wash 3 pint jars in hot soapy water; rinse well. Keep jars hot until needed.
9. Prepare lids according to manufacturer's directions.
10. Ladle the chutney into one jar at a time, leaving 1/4 inch headspace.
11. Release any trapped air bubbles by gently tapping the jar and using a clean, damp cloth to wipe the rim.
12. Attach the lid securely.
13. Place the filled jars in a canning pot or on a hot water bath.
14. Process the jars for 10 minutes in boiling water.
15. Remove jars from the water bath and allow them to cool on a towel.
16. Store the chutney in covered jars for at least 3 weeks before serving.
17. Garnish with an Italian parsley sprig, if desired, and serve as an accompaniment to curries or with crusty bread and cheese.
